goal: Wire SQLite cache for show + context --files + context --query, JSON view stays as fallback

summary.title: Wire SQLite cache for show + context --files + context --query (JSON fallback retained)
summary.what: Three new Store methods on the SQLite cache (ReadIntentViewByID / ReadIntentViewsByFiles / ReadIntentViewsByQuery), each returning ErrMainlineIndexUnavailable on cache miss so callers can fall through to JSON. engine.Show prefers SQLite primary-key lookup; engine.RetrieveContext picks its candidate set via a new candidateSetForRetrieval helper that goes through SQLite for --files/--query and stays on view.Intents for --current. Scoring math is unchanged.
summary.why: Per the architectural discussion: SQLite as read index, JSON as debuggable + schema-tolerant materialization output, source of truth in git refs. Today only mainline log read from SQLite; this PR wires the next three highest-leverage paths so the cache earns its place across the agent hot loop.

decisions.chose: JSON view is rebuilt on every sync; SQLite is rebuilt from JSON
decisions.rationale: JSON gives diffability + schema tolerance + fallback; SQLite gives index speed. Both have distinct value.
callers see byte-identical IntentView, so the only behaviour difference is speed. No risk of result drift between cache-present and cache-missing paths.
--current files come from the live diff, which varies turn-by-turn; the in-memory file-churn index already needs the full view, so SQLite would not save work for that mode.

decisions.chose: SQLite LIKE filters on the first non-stopword; multi-keyword scoring stays in memory
decisions.rationale: a single LIKE pattern is enough to shrink the corpus; multi-pattern in SQL is more code with diminishing returns at this scale (~hundreds of intents)

risks: SQLite raw_json round-trip relies on the column being valid JSON. If a future writer drops a field or stores a non-JSON value, the SQLite-backed read returns an unmarshal error and the caller may not fall through to JSON cleanly. Mitigation: writeMainlineIndex marshals iv directly so the contract is in one place.

anti_patterns.what: Letting SQLite-backed readers return a different shape than the JSON-view scan
anti_patterns.why: callers fall back to JSON on cache miss; if the two paths return different fields populated, behaviour silently changes when the cache is rebuilt
the JSON view is the diffable / inspectable / schema-tolerant materialization; SQLite alone loses those properties and offers no canonical-state win because git refs are the actual source of truth

followups: Wire mainline gaps to use intents.status filter via SQLite — current full-scan over view is fine for now but consistent with the pattern
Wire hub.BuildView to read the catalog via SQLite when present — preserves the same fallback shape
Consider an intent_anti_patterns query helper for a future status surface that flags hot anti-pattern surfaces across intents
